behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-handler handoff across local modules, not a single pure function. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no AI/ML output is involved. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is limited to "valid JSON payloads ... proceed through normal hook input handling" with no real-process or model-dependent requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real in-process hook input pipeline (stdin payload adapter/parser/handler) with a valid JSON fixture containing code fences, backticks, and $vars; assert parse success and downstream handler path executes expected normal outcome.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Level matches test: multi-module local wiring; no external infra.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: This checks interaction between parse failure mapping and trace-record construction/context fields. In-process module wiring is sufficient to catch real failures in emitted trace content. Rejected System because behavior does not require real process/stdin/exit semantics; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Feed malformed JSON into the real parser+error-mapper+trace-emitter stack in-process; assert emitted `json_parse_failed` record includes required diagnostic context (e.g., offset/snippet/error message fields).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Would not be reliable as pure Unit if trace context is assembled across modules.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about policy mapping plus telemetry intent marking, which is cross-module state handoff. No real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML dependency. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text says "local error-policy path."
behaviors.test_description: Trigger parse failure through the local hook input path and assert returned result is handled (no exception propagated) and telemetry-intent flag/event is set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is minimum to verify mapping + telemetry side effect together.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Real subprocess stdin, process stability, and pre-exit signal emission are OS/process-boundary behaviors. In-process fakes can miss crash/exit-order issues. Rejected Integration due explicit text "In real process execution" and stdin/exit semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed bytes to stdin, wait for exit, assert non-crash behavior (expected handled exit) and verify expected parse-failure trace signal is emitted before process termination.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real process/stdio lifecycle, so System is necessary.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end local process path starting at real stdin and producing real trace artifact via sink; boundary includes process I/O and sink behavior that mocks can hide. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)."
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook through its real invocation entrypoint with malformed stdin and assert the trace sink artifact (file/log/record) exists and contains the expected parse-failure trace type.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real invocation path and artifact emission, beyond in-process wiring.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Goal is classification/branch correctness across two payload classes through the hook input handling path; best caught with in-process module wiring and regression fixtures. Rejected System because no requirement for real subprocess/OS behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow because distinction is deterministic parser behavior, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired fixtures (valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings vs malformed JSON). Run both through integrated local input pipeline and assert only malformed case emits parse-failure trace while valid-markdown case follows success path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration needed to prevent false attribution across parser+error-routing behavior.
